editableNewDialogue.text: Quick science check: which change makes a new isotope—adding protons, neutrons, or electrons?
Adding neutrons. Protons change the element; electrons change the ion/charge.
And what’s the mass number counting again?
Mass number = protons + neutrons in the nucleus.
Now switching gears—can you rewrite a short bio blurb so it sounds confident but not over-the-top?
Yes—paste the blurb and tell me the vibe you want (friendly, flirty, funny, straightforward) and the length (1–2 lines, a short paragraph, or longer).
Make it a short paragraph, and keep it simple.

Got it. A good simple formula is: one line about what you’re like, one line about what you enjoy, one line inviting a message. For example: “Easygoing and upbeat. Always down to try a new spot or cook something good at home. If you’re looking for good conversation and a fun plan, say hi.”
Random: I read somewhere the first periodic table was in the 1800s—true?
Yes. Mendeleev published a widely recognized periodic table in 1869, and it helped predict elements that hadn’t been discovered yet.
Also I keep hearing about “the James Webb telescope images”—what’s the big deal?
JWST observes mostly in infrared, so it can see through dust and detect very distant, early-universe galaxies, plus detailed spectra of exoplanet atmospheres—big leap in sensitivity and resolution.
keep it short, and don’t put any emojis either—i’m trying to copy/paste it into Tinder without it looking corny.
Got you—short, clean, and no emojis. Want it more confident or more chill?
yo quick fix—when you rewite it, don’t put louis vuitton as my favorite. i’m not even into LV like that anymore.
Got you. I’ll take out Louis Vuitton and keep it more general (like “I’m into fashion” or “I like designer stuff”).
yeah, just keep it broad. no specific brand.
